headline: Oracle committee liveness can delay withdrawals/rebases (~100% TVS processing impact) but principal protected by sanity checks & dual-governance exit windows; no other material external deps

findings.text: Core contracts (Lido 0xae7ab96520DE3A18E5e111B5EaAb095312D7fE84, AccountingOracle 0x852deD011285fe67063a08005c71a85690503Cee) interact only with internal Lido contracts (via LidoLocator 0xC1d0b3DE6792Bf6b4b37EccdcC24e45978Cfd2Eb: Accounting, StakingRouter 0xFdDf38947aFB03C621C71b06C9C70bce73f12999, WithdrawalQueue 0x889edC2eDab5f40e902b864aD4d7AdE8E412F9B1, Burner, etc.) and the Ethereum Deposit Contract (substrate, per guardrails). No external price feeds (Chainlink/Pyth/Redstone), AMMs, or third-party lending pools called in core logic. Source: https://docs.lido.fi/contracts/accounting-oracle/ and Etherscan verified bytecode at AccountingOracle proxy.

Off-chain oracle committee (managed by HashConsensus 0xD624B08C83bAECF0807Dd2c6880C3154a5F0B288 for AccountingOracle and 0x7FaDB6358950c5fAA66Cb5EB8eE5147De3df355a for ValidatorsExitBusOracle) submits reports on validator balances, vault funds, exits, and withdrawal finalization. Committee is permissioned/DAO-managed with quorum (>50% of members); mis-reporting or liveness failure stalls reports (emits WarnProcessingMissed, skips frames) causing delayed rebases and withdrawal finalizations but reverts on invalid data via OracleReportSanityChecker 0xf1647c86E6D7959f638DD9CE1d90e2F3C9503129 (daily exit limits, extra-data bounds live/enforcing). Node operator set (via StakingRouter modules + CSM) is diversified (100+ independent operators); validator slashing is base-chain substrate (not counted per guardrails). No principal loss possible. Evidence: https://docs.lido.fi/contracts/accounting-oracle/, https://etherscan.io/address/0x852deD011285fe67063a08005c71a85690503Cee, recent oracle audits (Certora 12-2025, MixBytes 03-2026).

No material bridge/cross-chain messaging dependencies for core user flows or TVS. Non-primary chains (Solana, Moonbeam, Moonriver, Terra) show negligible TVL (<0.02% combined; Solana ~$4M residual post-2023 exit, others <$50k) per 2026 data; wstETH on L2s is user-initiated bridging without protocol trust assumption or core contract calls. Core staking/withdrawals remain Ethereum-native. Retired/sunset deployments omitted. Evidence: https://docs.lido.fi/deployed-contracts/ (Ethereum-only detailed), DeFiLlama TVL breakdown cross-checked via search.

No nested collateral/restaking chains in core design. Lido performs direct staking to Ethereum Beacon Chain via Deposit Contract; stETH/wstETH receipt tokens are base-layer LSTs. No LRT-of-LRT or multi-level slashing propagation in protocol contracts. Downstream restaking (e.g., EigenLayer on stETH) is not trusted or called by Lido. Evidence: https://docs.lido.fi/contracts/lido/ and AccountingOracle source interactions (no restaking primitives).

No fork lineage. Lido is the original liquid staking protocol (launched 2020); DeFiLlama forkedFrom empty. Evidence: protocol history in https://docs.lido.fi/ and GitHub lidofinance/core (no fork references in v3.0.2 structure).

Live fallbacks/circuit breakers: OracleReportSanityChecker enforces rebase/exit bounds and reverts bad reports; bunker mode flag in reports for adverse conditions; missed reports skip frames with warnings (no invalid state applied); quorum on HashConsensus prevents single-oracle dominance. All (i) LIVE and enforcing on-chain today (proxies active per Etherscan, recent reports processed). These mitigate A2 oracle risks to stall-only (no principal loss or unmitigated mispricing). No (ii)/(iii) unactivated paths cited for grade. Evidence: https://docs.lido.fi/contracts/accounting-oracle/ (sanity checks, bunker, simulateOracleReport), OracleReportSanityChecker address 0xf1647c86E6D7959f638DD9CE1d90e2F3C9503129 on Etherscan.

No sequencer/L1-liveness dependency beyond Ethereum base-chain substrate. All core contracts (Lido, oracles, router) deployed on Ethereum L1; no L2 sequencer trust or DA committee for primary flows. L2 wstETH deployments are peripheral/user-facing. Evidence: https://docs.lido.fi/deployed-contracts/ (Ethereum Mainnet focus) and Etherscan addresses for all listed proxies.

No critical permissionless keeper/relayer bots required for core functions. Oracle reports submitted by DAO-managed committee; validator duties performed by diversified node operators (StakingRouter + CSM, 100+ operators with bonding/slashing). No auto-compound, liquidation, or deposit-relayer liveness failure mode that causes insolvency or stale positions. Failure degrades gracefully to manual/queued processing. Evidence: https://docs.lido.fi/guides/oracle-operator-manual/, StakingRouter 0xFdDf38947aFB03C621C71b06C9C70bce73f12999 on Etherscan.

Lido DAO (Aragon Kernel + Dual Governance) can mutate oracle members (HashConsensus), add/register staking modules, or alter dependencies, but all executions subject to minimum 3-day timelock + dynamic veto signaling (1-10%+ stETH in escrow extends delay 5-45+ days, enabling user exits via withdrawal queue). Provides explicit exit window; no hot-swap or silent introduction. Dual Governance live/enforcing. Evidence: https://docs.lido.fi/guides/dg-guide (3-day timelock, veto mechanics), Lido DAO Agent 0x3e40D73EB977Dc6a537aF587D48316feE66E9C8c on Etherscan, recent governance proposals.

steelman.red: The permissioned oracle committee (A2) is a single external point whose coordinated liveness failure or quorum-captured misreport could indefinitely stall all rebases and withdrawal finalizations across 100% TVS with only delay-based mitigations, and DAO control over modules/oracles (A9) carries governance risk even with timelocks, warranting red for material external dependency exposure.
steelman.orange: Oracle committee liveness failure or disagreement (A2) can materially pause expected performance by delaying withdrawal finalizations and rebase application for the entire TVS, and while sanity checks (A6) and dual-governance exit windows (A9, 3d+ veto) prevent principal loss or silent changes, the absence of a live permissionless fallback that fully preserves timely access makes orange the accurate classification over green.
steelman.green: Diversified node operators, on-chain quorum-enforced oracle consensus with live sanity limits and bunker mode (A6), complete independence from non-substrate externals (A1,A3,A4,A7,A8), and strong dual-governance protections providing multi-day user exit windows for any DAO mutation (A9) ensure external failures cause only temporary recoverable delays with zero permanent principal or yield loss, qualifying the protocol as green autonomy.

rationale.verdict: Choosing orange because the concrete A2 oracle committee (HashConsensus 0xD624B08C83bAECF0807Dd2c6880C3154a5F0B288, quorum-based, DAO-managed) plus A6 live sanity checker can cause temporary but material performance degradation (paused finalizations, delayed rebases) across ~100% TVS without a fully live alternative preserving expected timely access (distinguishing from green), yet on-chain reverts, base-chain substrate, and dual-governance 3-day+ veto/exit windows (A9, stETH-holder signaling) eliminate principal loss or unmitigated governance risk (stronger steelman than red); all backed by Etherscan proxies (Lido 0xae7ab96520DE3A18E5e111B5EaAb095312D7fE84, AccountingOracle 0x852deD011285fe67063a08005c71a85690503Cee), https://docs.lido.fi/contracts/accounting-oracle/, https://docs.lido.fi/deployed-contracts/, and https://docs.lido.fi/guides/dg-guide.

evidence.shows: Deployed Lido/stETH proxy and verified implementation; confirms on-chain state for core staking logic and interactions.
AccountingOracle proxy (receives oracle reports, applies via Accounting); live sanity checks and report processing visible in verified source and recent txs.
HashConsensus for AccountingOracle (manages oracle committee, quorum, report hashing); on-chain evidence of A2 committee mechanism.
Canonical list of all production addresses (LidoLocator, StakingRouter, oracles, WithdrawalQueue, etc.) confirming Ethereum-primary deployment and no material non-Eth bridges in core.
Detailed architecture of oracle reporting, sanity checks (OracleReportSanityChecker), bunker mode, quorum process, missed-report handling, and internal contract calls only.
Recent audits (Certora Lido V3 Oracle V7 12-2025, MixBytes 03-2026, Ackee CSM/StakingRouter 09-2025) confirming oracle and core components with findings fixed/acknowledged; no unmitigated criticals.
Dual Governance mechanics: 3-day minimum timelock + dynamic veto signaling (stETH escrow) providing exit window for governance actions (A9).
Active public bug bounty program (up to $2M) for smart contracts and apps.

unknowns: A2: exact current oracle member count and quorum threshold value on HashConsensus 0xD624B08C83bAECF0807Dd2c6880C3154a5F0B288 (readContract dynamic data not retrievable via available tools; historical ~5-9 with >50% quorum from docs)

protocol_metadata.about: Lido enables users to stake ETH on Ethereum and receive stETH (rebasing liquid staking token) or wstETH (wrapped non-rebasing variant) that accrues Beacon Chain rewards. It routes deposits through a diversified set of node operators via the StakingRouter, uses an on-chain oracle committee (HashConsensus + AccountingOracle) for validator balance/exit reporting and withdrawal finalization, and supports broad DeFi composability as collateral while maintaining user exit rights via a withdrawal queue.
